title: achillea rupestris subsp. rupestris
intro: Achillea rupestris subsp. rupestris, also known as Alpine Yarrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Achillea. It is a low-growing perennial herb with fern-like foliage and small white flowers, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Achillea rupestris subsp. rupestris include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the plant's essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues, and it can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses.
